title: Replace Offboarded Assignee
excerpt:

posts.author: MarigoldsInMay (Darielle Shandler)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

When we offboard an employee and they are tagged in many boards across the account, it can become a burden to catch each place they are tagged, or automated to be tagged in a future item created. It would be wonderful to be able to replace anywhere Person 1 is tagged with Person 2. More specifically, after their account has been deactivated. So with a single click, I could replace the grayed out tagged account with a new employee.

What are you trying to achieve

Being able to quickly replace where an offboarded employee is tagged with a new employee.

posts.author: taylorbrooks (Taylor Brooks)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 to this. The missing piece is account-level reassignment, not just board-level cleanup.

The workaround I have seen teams use is:

  1. query every board where the offboarded user still appears in a People column or in automation recipes
  2. bulk replace them board by board
  3. separately update templates and any automations that still point at the old user

That works, but it is messy, and it is easy to miss future automations or archived boards that get reused later.

A practical version of this feature would be an admin action like Replace user assignments, where monday updates:

  • People columns
  • board owners
  • item owners
  • automation recipients and assignees
  • templates using that user reference

Even if it started as an admin-only tool with a confirmation screen, it would save a lot of cleanup during offboarding.
